title: Inequality in Évian
content: The G7 summit must recognize that extreme disparities in income, wealth, opportunity, and political influence are not only a defining feature of the global economy but also a major factor in many other shared challenges. For too long, the issue has been treated as a symptom rather than a cause.
snippet: The G7 summit must recognize that extreme disparities in income, wealth, opportunity, and political influence are not only a defining feature of the global economy but also a major factor in many
creator: Adriana Abdenur
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Europeans Are the G7’s Grownups
content: Slowly but surely, the European Union is taking steps to reduce its current-account surplus and direct more of its substantial savings into domestic investment and growth. Unfortunately, at the G7 Summit in France this month, the United States will refuse to address global imbalances, including its own.
snippet: Slowly but surely, the European Union is taking steps to reduce its current-account surplus and direct more of its substantial savings into domestic investment and growth. Unfortunately, at the G7
creator: Laura Tyson
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Modi Is Rigging Indian Democracy
content: Under the guise of routine voter-roll maintenance, India’s Election Commission has purged tens of millions of voters, disproportionately affecting opposition strongholds, the poor, and Muslim minorities. The result is an electoral system increasingly designed to favor the ruling Bharatiya Janata Party.
snippet: Under the guise of routine voter-roll maintenance, India’s Election Commission has purged tens of millions of voters, disproportionately affecting opposition strongholds, the poor, and Muslim
creator: Jayati Ghosh
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Learning to Govern a Fragmented World
content: As the postwar multilateral order gives way to a multipolar and more contested world, global governance must adapt or risk irrelevance. The upcoming G7 summit offers a unique opportunity to champion a coalition-based approach that translates shared interests into collective action.
snippet: As the postwar multilateral order gives way to a multipolar and more contested world, global governance must adapt or risk irrelevance. The upcoming G7 summit offers a unique opportunity to champion a
creator: Dennis J. Snower
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Cognition for Sale
content: AI is doing what no previous technology could, turning tacit human knowledge into a product that can be extracted, priced, and distributed at scale. Yet the same technology that democratizes cognitive labor may also weaken the human capacities on which complex societies depend.
snippet: AI is doing what no previous technology could, turning tacit human knowledge into a product that can be extracted, priced, and distributed at scale. Yet the same technology that democratizes cognitive
creator: Sami Mahroum
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Big Tech Gets Bigger
content: Recent stock-market gyrations are a reminder of just how dependent the United States and global economies have become on investors’ seemingly insatiable appetite for tech stocks. Even if a major correction is in the offing, however, it will not happen before SpaceX’s massive initial public offering draws in yet more leveraged investment, nor will it destroy the market power of the other tech giants that have spent years perfecting anti-competitive tactics that largely escape regulators’ purview.
snippet: Recent stock-market gyrations are a reminder of just how dependent the United States and global economies have become on investors’ seemingly insatiable appetite for tech stocks. Even if a major
creator: PS Commentators
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Big Picture
creator: PS editors
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Democrats Need a Story, Not More Lists
content: Whereas Donald Trump has offered voters a clear and compelling narrative, Democrats have provided only vague aspirations and long lists of policies. To win over voters—rather than scrounging protest votes—they must articulate a compelling economic vision, with wage-led growth as its foundation.
snippet: Whereas Donald Trump has offered voters a clear and compelling narrative, Democrats have provided only vague aspirations and long lists of policies. To win over voters—rather than scrounging protest
creator: Teresa Ghilarducci
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What if Europe Loses Access to Its Climate Data?
content: The European Green Deal runs on data, from corporate disclosures to climate-risk analyses and decarbonization pathways. But much of this data feeds platforms and tools developed outside the European Union, which could threaten the bloc’s climate agenda and even its foundational values.
snippet: The European Green Deal runs on data, from corporate disclosures to climate-risk analyses and decarbonization pathways. But much of this data feeds platforms and tools developed outside the European
creator: Bertrand Badré
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Raising Interest Rates Won’t Stabilize African Inflation
content: Monetary tightening in Africa would not only fail to rein in inflation; it would raise the cost of productive investment, suppress growth, and hamper the continent's much-needed structural transformation. A better strategy would focus on accelerating this transformation by channeling finance into productive industries.
snippet: Monetary tightening in Africa would not only fail to rein in inflation; it would raise the cost of productive investment, suppress growth, and hamper the continent's much-needed structural
creator: Célestin Monga
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Enduring Enigma of Economic Growth
content: Developing countries have made enormous progress in many of the factors behind growth, reflected in stronger institutions, rising education levels, higher life expectancy, and increased investment rates. Yet income convergence with advanced economies has been painfully slow—and in most cases absent.
snippet: Developing countries have made enormous progress in many of the factors behind growth, reflected in stronger institutions, rising education levels, higher life expectancy, and increased investment
creator: Andrés Velasco
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Is the US Stock Market Too Big?
content: With China and India having grown so fast as to compensate for the underperformance of their fellow BRICS (Brazil, Russia, and South Africa), the center of gravity in the global economy continues to shift eastward. The question now is whether—and when—equity valuations will reflect this underlying reality.
snippet: With China and India having grown so fast as to compensate for the underperformance of their fellow BRICS (Brazil, Russia, and South Africa), the center of gravity in the global economy continues to
creator: Jim O'Neill
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Brazil’s Bad Vibes
content: A government that abandons structural agendas whenever sentiment turns negative will still be managing sentiment four years later—with nothing to show for it. Brazil’s “vibecession” is a real problem for the current government; but winning the news cycle is not a real solution.
snippet: A government that abandons structural agendas whenever sentiment turns negative will still be managing sentiment four years later—with nothing to show for it. Brazil’s “vibecession” is a real problem
creator: Laura Carvalho
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Negotiating With the Antichrist
content: Apocalyptic thinking is seeping into the political discourse, with figures like Peter Thiel, Pete Hegseth, and Aleksandr Dugin portraying political differences as struggles between good and evil. When rivals become mortal enemies, the instruments of politics and diplomacy cannot work.
snippet: Apocalyptic thinking is seeping into the political discourse, with figures like Peter Thiel, Pete Hegseth, and Aleksandr Dugin portraying political differences as struggles between good and evil. When
creator: Nina L. Khrushcheva
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Is Europe’s Economy Falling Short?
content: For much of the postwar era, European policymakers have prioritized stability and predictability over fostering breakthrough innovation. Today, as the United States and China compete for AI dominance, a stagnant Europe is struggling to regain its place at the technological frontier.
snippet: For much of the postwar era, European policymakers have prioritized stability and predictability over fostering breakthrough innovation. Today, as the United States and China compete for AI dominance,
creator: Philippe Aghion
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Big Tech’s Shadow Market Power
content: Big Tech firms are amassing market power through strategies that bypass current rules for oversight, investigation, and intervention. Unless and until regulators account for the full scope and scale of these strategies, Silicon Valley’s dominance will remain unchallenged.
snippet: Big Tech firms are amassing market power through strategies that bypass current rules for oversight, investigation, and intervention. Unless and until regulators account for the full scope and scale
creator: Helena Malikova
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The AI Revolution Mirrors the Green Transition
content: The need for massive upfront investments and the likelihood of significant job displacement imply remarkable parallels between the AI buildout and the green transition. In both cases, the state has an important role to play in guiding market forces on behalf of the public good.
snippet: The need for massive upfront investments and the likelihood of significant job displacement imply remarkable parallels between the AI buildout and the green transition. In both cases, the state has an
creator: Adam Michael Bauer
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Where Is China’s Trillion-Dollar Trade Surplus Going?
content: Whereas China’s trade surplus used to be absorbed largely by the central bank and channeled into official reserves, it is now being redeployed by private actors in search of yield. This indicates China’s progress on financial deepening and underscores markets' increasingly central role in shaping the economy.
snippet: Whereas China’s trade surplus used to be absorbed largely by the central bank and channeled into official reserves, it is now being redeployed by private actors in search of yield. This indicates
creator: Miao Yanliang
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Future of Development Finance Is Not Primarily About Money
content: Multilateral development banks have not kept pace with changes to the global financial environment. For middle-income countries like India, accessing knowledge and technology is now a bigger challenge than raising capital, which suggests that these institutions must rethink their operating model.
snippet: Multilateral development banks have not kept pace with changes to the global financial environment. For middle-income countries like India, accessing knowledge and technology is now a bigger challenge
creator: Tanu M. Goyal
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: No Bridge Over Healing Waters for the G7
content: The French G7 presidency’s focus on global imbalances is both welcome and problematic. While long-standing trade imbalances between the United States, Europe, and China are, if anything, becoming even more troublesome, the leaders’ summit in Évian-les-Bains will do nothing to address the problem.
snippet: The French G7 presidency’s focus on global imbalances is both welcome and problematic. While long-standing trade imbalances between the United States, Europe, and China are, if anything, becoming even
creator: Barry Eichengreen
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Are Government Stakes the Key to AI Sovereignty?
content: At first glance, reports that the US and Chinese governments are both considering taking stakes in national AI champions would seem to suggest a convergence between the world’s two leading AI powers. But a closer look reveals stark differences in their underlying strategies.
snippet: At first glance, reports that the US and Chinese governments are both considering taking stakes in national AI champions would seem to suggest a convergence between the world’s two leading AI powers.
creator: Angela Huyue Zhang
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What Next for Global Trade?
creator: Pinelopi Koujianou Goldberg
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: From the American Revolution to Universal Suffrage
content: Britain’s attempt to crush the American Revolution exposed a constitutional crisis at the heart of its empire. One English statesman saw in it proof that only broader suffrage could check royal power, tame corruption, and ensure that imperial rule remained compatible with liberty.
snippet: Britain’s attempt to crush the American Revolution exposed a constitutional crisis at the heart of its empire. One English statesman saw in it proof that only broader suffrage could check royal power,
creator: Danielle Allen
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Jefferson’s Revolutionary Language
content: Of the delegates who assembled in Philadelphia in 1776 for the Second Continental Congress, only Thomas Jefferson possessed the skill, knowledge, and linguistic gifts to make the Declaration of Independence an immortal text. He was the self-appointed conscience of America, and sensational imagery was his stock-in-trade.
snippet: Of the delegates who assembled in Philadelphia in 1776 for the Second Continental Congress, only Thomas Jefferson possessed the skill, knowledge, and linguistic gifts to make the Declaration of
creator: Andrew Burstein
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Declaration of the Enslaved
content: For African-Americans, the Declaration of Independence was nothing but a “thin veil to cover the crimes” of slavery, as Frederick Douglass put it. Inspired by Haiti’s Declaration of Independence, free and enslaved Black people in the US found themselves subverting America’s founding document to achieve their own emancipation.
snippet: For African-Americans, the Declaration of Independence was nothing but a “thin veil to cover the crimes” of slavery, as Frederick Douglass put it. Inspired by Haiti’s Declaration of Independence, free
creator: Sudhir Hazareesingh
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Free-Market Capitalism Is Destroying US Democracy
content: Far from two sides of the same coin, free-market capitalism and democracy are fundamentally incompatible. The only way to restore US democracy is to transform the system, so that it compensates innovators but limits their market power, while ensuring that the benefits of economic progress are more equitably shared.
snippet: Far from two sides of the same coin, free-market capitalism and democracy are fundamentally incompatible. The only way to restore US democracy is to transform the system, so that it compensates
creator: Mordecai Kurz
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Canada’s Great Recalibration
content: The growing fusion of economics, security, and technology has altered the nature of 21st-century interdependence, and Canada has responded by being more intentional. Rather than automatically serving as an extension of US-centered systems, it is creating more options across a wider network of relationships.
snippet: The growing fusion of economics, security, and technology has altered the nature of 21st-century interdependence, and Canada has responded by being more intentional. Rather than automatically serving
creator: Paul Samson
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: From Jefferson to Nehru
content: The Declaration of Independence continues to challenge countries, including the US and India, to measure themselves against the ideals they profess. But in a century marked by rising inequality, an authoritarian resurgence, and the emergence of new forms of domination, measuring up will become only more difficult.
snippet: The Declaration of Independence continues to challenge countries, including the US and India, to measure themselves against the ideals they profess. But in a century marked by rising inequality, an
creator: Shashi Tharoor
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Roots of Democratic Decline
content: Notwithstanding recent setbacks for illiberal, populist, and authoritarian leaders in some countries, good-governance groups and experts on democratic decline continue to document a disturbing global trend toward “autocratization.” What remains to be seen is whether enough voters will wake up to the threat.
snippet: Notwithstanding recent setbacks for illiberal, populist, and authoritarian leaders in some countries, good-governance groups and experts on democratic decline continue to document a disturbing global
creator: Zehra F. Kabasakal Arat
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Standard of Independence
content: Although revolutions would surely have occurred had the United States never broken from Britain in 1776, the Declaration of Independence gave later anticolonial movements inspiration and the language to shape their own aspirations. It offered a universal blueprint, even as the US itself adopted the ethos of empire.
snippet: Although revolutions would surely have occurred had the United States never broken from Britain in 1776, the Declaration of Independence gave later anticolonial movements inspiration and the language
creator: David Armitage
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Declaration of Independence Turns South
content: Whether they were agreeing or arguing, the new countries that emerged across the Americas after the American Revolution influenced each other. Together, they helped shape the global development of modern democracy and the social and political movements that have shaped it ever since.
snippet: Whether they were agreeing or arguing, the new countries that emerged across the Americas after the American Revolution influenced each other. Together, they helped shape the global development of
creator: Caitlin Fitz
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Capitalism’s Creature
content: The American Dream, with its basis in Thomas Jefferson’s immortal words, was one of the most powerfully generative ideas ever conceived. But the economy and impersonal systems it helped to bring into being are now shaping society and its individual members, rather than the other way around.
snippet: The American Dream, with its basis in Thomas Jefferson’s immortal words, was one of the most powerfully generative ideas ever conceived. But the economy and impersonal systems it helped to bring into
creator: Antara Haldar
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What the Declaration Did and Did Not Say
content: The American founders’ understanding of political community was ethnically and racially restrictive in ways that Americans have since repudiated. But the Declaration of Independence’s underlying logic and treatment of rights and what it takes to secure them remains as cogent as it was 250 years ago.
snippet: The American founders’ understanding of political community was ethnically and racially restrictive in ways that Americans have since repudiated. But the Declaration of Independence’s underlying logic
creator: Stephen Holmes
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Independence for Me, But Not for Thee
content: The Declaration of Independence spoke of all states as being "separate and equal," reflecting the founders' deference to the norms of international law and practice. Yet 250 years later, Donald Trump's administration has inverted this posture, much to the detriment of America's standing in the world.
snippet: The Declaration of Independence spoke of all states as being "separate and equal," reflecting the founders' deference to the norms of international law and practice. Yet 250 years later, Donald
creator: Aziz Huq
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: China’s AI Agenda
content: While American and international news coverage of AI tends to focus on a handful of Silicon Valley labs, China's more pragmatic approach to leveraging the technology could ultimately be the bigger story. What the Chinese leadership sees in the technology is nothing less than a master key for its economic ambitions.
snippet: While American and international news coverage of AI tends to focus on a handful of Silicon Valley labs, China's more pragmatic approach to leveraging the technology could ultimately be the bigger
creator: Lizzi C. Lee
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: After the Liberal Order
content: Although the liberal international order was always hierarchical, it was defensible because it allowed for restraint, negotiation, and benefits extending beyond the inner circle. As that internal moderating project fails, all that will remain is the old hierarchy and whatever resentments it engenders.
snippet: Although the liberal international order was always hierarchical, it was defensible because it allowed for restraint, negotiation, and benefits extending beyond the inner circle. As that internal
creator: Carla Norrlöf
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Declaration of Independence and Indigenous Nations
content: America’s swaggering foreign policy, as displayed most recently in US President Donald Trump’s unprovoked bombing campaign against Iran, can be traced back to its founding. The Declaration of Independence’s vitriol toward Indigenous people illustrates how conquest underpinned the ideal of liberty.
snippet: America’s swaggering foreign policy, as displayed most recently in US President Donald Trump’s unprovoked bombing campaign against Iran, can be traced back to its founding. The Declaration of
creator: Sarah M.S. Pearsall
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: America’s Founders vs. Oligarchy
content: US President Donald Trump's administration argues that restoring America's former "greatness" requires a rejection of immigration, the aggressive use of tariffs, and no limits on the concentration of wealth. The Declaration of Independence—and the Patriot vision on which it was based—argues precisely the opposite.

creator: Steve Pincus
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page
